title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-23 · 纽约初选与民主社会主义者崛起
summary: 纽约初选日，市长Zohran Mamdani支持的民主社会主义者挑战三位现任民主党众议员，考验其政治影响力。最高法院即将裁决关键案件，可能界定总统权力。情报机构开始解雇所谓'深层政府'人员。塔克·卡尔森宣布不再支持共和党。节目嘉宾讨论民主社会主义者崛起、党内分裂及AIPAC影响。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.en: And imagine opening your door and finding two million reasons to stay inside. That is exactly what happened in Texas after a truck carrying millions of bees crashed. Video from Queen Bee Supply shows the chaos workers faced to capture the swarm. The company says that professional and backyard beekeepers stepped in to help. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. We're buzzing away. But CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 想象一下，打开门发现有两百万个理由让你待在家里。这正是德克萨斯州发生的事，一辆载有数百万只蜜蜂的卡车发生事故。Queen Bee Supply 的视频显示了工人们捕捉蜂群时面临的混乱场面。该公司表示，专业养蜂人和业余养蜂人都伸出援手。CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS 就到这里。我是布拉德·史密斯。我们嗡嗡地飞走了。但 CNN THIS MORNING 与奥黛·科尼什的节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today in the group chat, it's primary day in New York. Can more Democratic socialists ride Mayor Mamdani's wave? MAYOR ZOHRAN MAMDANI (D),
paragraphs.cn: 今天在群聊中，是纽约的初选日。更多的民主社会主义者能乘着市长曼达尼的浪潮前进吗？市长佐兰·曼达尼（民主党）

paragraphs.speaker: New York City
paragraphs.en: When does the race for 2028 begin? It starts now.
paragraphs.cn: 2028年的竞选何时开始？现在就开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Anticipation builds to today's Supreme Court decisions. Will justices go against the president in cases that could help define his power? And so-called deep-state firings have started at a top U.S. intelligence agency. What else could the president's hand-picked temporary director do? And Tucker Carlson says he's out. Will he take MAGA with him?
paragraphs.cn: 对今天最高法院裁决的期待在增加。法官们会在可能有助于界定总统权力的案件中反对总统吗？所谓的深层政府解雇行动已在美国一家顶级情报机构开始。总统亲自挑选的临时主任还能做什么？塔克·卡尔森说他出局了。他会带走MAGA吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Tucker Carlson
paragraphs.en: I would not support the Republican Party. There's no chance I would support the Republican Party.
paragraphs.cn: 我不会支持共和党。我绝无可能支持共和党。

paragraphs.speaker: Mamdani
paragraphs.en: Now, people often ask me what I think of the state of the Democratic Party. This slate here today is our answer. The Democratic Party must change.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，人们常问我如何看待民主党的现状。今天这里的名单就是我们的答案。民主党必须改变。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Zohran Mamdani putting his power to the test. Can his team of Democratic socialists win big in New York? Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish, and right now polls are open in New York. And the influence of New York City's mayor, tested just six months into his tenure. He's backed candidates in three House races. Two are going up against Democratic incumbents, and they are hoping to ride the wave of Democratic socialists, who have been winning big races across the country. BRAD LANDER (D),
paragraphs.cn: 佐兰·曼达尼正在考验他的实力。他的民主社会主义者团队能在纽约大获全胜吗？大家早上好。我是奥黛·科尼什，现在纽约的投票站已经开放。纽约市市长的影响力，在其上任仅六个月后就受到考验。他支持了三个众议院选区的候选人。其中两人正与民主党现任议员竞争，他们希望乘着民主社会主义者的浪潮前进，这些人已在全美赢得重大选举。布拉德·兰德（民主党）

paragraphs.speaker: Candidate
paragraphs.en: I'm a longtime progressive, a Working Families Party member. But yes, if by "Democratic socialist," do you mean do I want Medicare for all? Do I want to make sure that folks can organize unions? Do I want to make sure we're getting to universal childcare and investing a lot more in housing everyone can afford? Yes. DARIALIZA AVILA CHEVALIER (D),
paragraphs.cn: 我是一名长期进步派，是工人家庭党成员。但是的，如果"民主社会主义者"是指我是否想要全民医保？我是否想要确保人们能够组织工会？我是否想要确保我们实现全民托儿服务并大力投资于人人负担得起的住房？是的。达里亚利扎·阿维拉·切瓦利尔（民主党）

paragraphs.speaker: Candidate
paragraphs.en: I am someone who has felt deeply abandoned by the establishment politics that far too often see my community as merely a statistic.
paragraphs.cn: 我是一个深感被建制政治抛弃的人，这种政治常常把我的社区仅仅视为统计数据。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, so today in the group chat, Tamara Keith, senior political correspondent at NPR; Chuck Rocha, Democratic strategist and former senior adviser to Bernie Sanders's presidential campaigns; and Ashley Davis, former White House official under President George W. Bush. Love seeing you guys. Thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，今天在群聊中，有NPR高级政治记者塔玛拉·基思；民主党策略师、伯尼·桑德斯总统竞选前高级顾问查克·罗查；以及前乔治·W·布什总统白宫官员阿什莉·戴维斯。很高兴见到你们。非常感谢你们的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, Tamara, I want to start with you, because Mamdani story is a story that I think a lot of these folks would like to emulate. They're not handpicked by the party, yet somehow, they rise via the -- you know, the Internet and the power of their ideas. And he is trying to have, like, tailwinds. So, do we know if he does?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，塔玛拉，我想先问你，因为曼达尼的故事是很多人想要效仿的。他们不是由政党挑选的，但不知何故，他们通过互联网和思想的力量崛起。他正试图获得顺风。那么，我们知道他是否成功了吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Tamara Keith
paragraphs.en: I think we will know very soon whether he does. He -- certainly, some of these candidates look to be polling pretty well in their races. I don't know that we can extrapolate about the national Democratic Party from what happens in New York City.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为我们很快就会知道。他 -- -- 当然，其中一些候选人在他们的竞选中民调表现不错。我不知道我们能否从纽约市发生的事情推断出全国民主党的情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No, I mean, Democratic socialists.
paragraphs.cn: 不，我是说，民主社会主义者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Which, city by city, I mean, even here in
paragraphs.cn: 每个城市，我的意思是，即使在这里 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- we've been talking about that. I was noticing in -- was that Lander who we just played earlier?
paragraphs.cn: 我们一直在讨论这个。我注意到 -- -- 那是我们刚才播放的兰德吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Notice how the first thing out of his mouth in that answer is, "If by Democratic socialist, you mean." And I hear this over and over again, people trying to define it for themselves, even as actual Democratic socialists are out there defining it pretty loudly.
paragraphs.cn: 注意他回答时脱口而出的第一句话是："如果民主社会主义者是指……"我一次又一次地听到这个，人们试图为自己定义它，即使真正的民主社会主义者正在大声地定义它。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: I would go a step further and say that it's already seen. In political world, you don't beat incumbents. You don't go against the machine. In city politics, New York City. To your point, Audie, in Washington, D.C., just last week, when three Democratic socialists overcame the odds in like --
paragraphs.cn: 我想更进一步说，这已经看到了。在政治世界里，你不会击败现任者。你不会对抗体制。在城市政治中，纽约市。正如奥迪所说，在华盛顿特区，就在上周，当三位民主社会主义者克服困难时 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: but that's much different than every other battleground district in America. There's about 34 battleground districts, and none of those are in the heart of any city where Democratic socialists -- And that's what the party is worried about. The Democratic socialist message of being for the people, by the people. Working class. Pull yourself up. That's all good. But in these marginal seats, they'll say everybody's a Democratic socialist. All Ashley's friends will be running ads tomorrow on this.
paragraphs.cn: 但这与美国其他每一个战场选区都大不相同。大约有34个战场选区，没有一个位于民主社会主义者所在的任何城市中心 -- -- 而这正是政党所担心的。民主社会主义者"民有、民治"的信息，工人阶级，自力更生，这些都很好。但在这些边缘席位中，他们会说每个人都是民主社会主义者。所有阿什利的朋友明天都会为此投放广告。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I know. You're trying to divorce it here. I want to play for you. This is Attorney General Tish James.
paragraphs.cn: 我知道。你想在这里撇清关系。我想为你播放一段。这是司法部长蒂什·詹姆斯。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: -- with it. I ain't divorcing nothing.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 和它一起。我什么都没撇清。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No, no, hold on one second, because this is going to be the difficult thing as a consultant.
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，等一下，因为作为顾问，这将是困难的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: How do you do this when there are people who are successfully running --
paragraphs.cn: 当有人成功运作时，你该如何做到 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- behind this flag? So basically, race was -- Vox was calling this race a splitting of Mamdani's coalition: that it pitted progressives against Democratic socialists. And then Letitia James was asked about this split. She's the former [SIC] attorney general. Here's what she had to say.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 在这面旗帜背后？所以基本上，这场竞选 -- -- Vox称这场竞选是曼达尼联盟的分裂：它让进步派与民主社会主义者对立。然后莱蒂西亚·詹姆斯被问及这一分裂。她是前[原文如此]总检察长。以下是她的说法。

paragraphs.speaker: Letitia James
paragraphs.en: What we are witnessing here at this moment is the tension. Dr. King talked about creative tension being a catalyst for social -- for social justice. And clearly, given D.S. -- DSA and quote, unquote "progressive Democrats," we are moving forward, and we are broadening the tent of the Democratic Party. We are moving forward, broadening the tent, focusing on women, people of color, immigrants, working people in the labor movement.
paragraphs.cn: 我们此刻在这里目睹的是紧张局势。金博士谈到创造性紧张是社会正义的催化剂。显然，鉴于D.S. -- -- DSA和所谓的"进步派民主党人"，我们正在前进，我们正在扩大民主党的帐篷。我们正在前进，扩大帐篷，关注女性、有色人种、移民、劳工运动中的工人。

paragraphs.speaker: Letitia James
paragraphs.en: And that's why -- that's what you are witnessing now, given the split between a mayor who I've supported and who is a friend of mine, who I respect.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是为什么 -- -- 这就是你现在所目睹的，鉴于一位我曾支持、也是我的朋友、我尊敬的市长之间的分裂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And I should say current attorney general.
paragraphs.cn: 我应该说现任总检察长。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But I wanted to ask you how you hear her describing this split, which I think Bush-era Republicans may be familiar with. Right? When the party starts to pull away from you.
paragraphs.cn: 但我想问你，你如何理解她描述的这种分裂？我认为布什时代的共和党人可能对此很熟悉。对吧？当政党开始远离你时。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Well, right. I mean, I think this whole movement is two things. One, it's the Tea Party of the Republicans back in the day, or maybe even MAGA in some way. But also, I think it's really anti-establishment, which most people in the country, whether you are Republicans or Democrats, are 100 percent running against the status quo, because no one's happy with it. But what's really going to show me for the mayor, because I think that these -- he has a really good chance of winning these three races today.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，对。我的意思是，我认为整个运动有两方面。一是当年共和党的茶党，或者甚至某种程度上是MAGA。但我也认为这真的是反建制，全国大多数人，无论你是共和党还是民主党，都百分之百反对现状，因为没有人对现状满意。但真正让我关注市长的是，因为我认为这些 -- -- 他今天赢得这三场竞选的机会很大。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: At least two of them. But can they win in a general? And I think that's the question.
paragraphs.cn: 至少其中两场。但他们能在普选中获胜吗？我认为这是个问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: I think we'll see. Some will, some won't.
paragraphs.cn: 我想我们会看到的。有些人会，有些人不会。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me just play one last thing. This is the mayor. Mayor Zohran Mamdani. He was just speaking from the mayor's office on Monday.
paragraphs.cn: 最后再放一段。这是市长佐兰·马姆达尼。他周一刚从市长办公室发表讲话。

paragraphs.speaker: Mamdani
paragraphs.en: I was quoting Gramsci, who said that the old world is dying and the new world struggles to be born. Now is the time of monsters. I used the term to describe all those who are preventing the birth of a new world. Not solely AIPAC, but frankly, super PACs at large who are spending millions of dollars in deceptive and misleading ads that are blanketing airwaves.
paragraphs.cn: 我引用了葛兰西的话，他说旧世界正在消亡，新世界挣扎着诞生。现在是怪物的时代。我用这个词来形容所有阻碍新世界诞生的人。不仅仅是AIPAC，坦率地说，还有那些花费数百万美元投放欺骗性和误导性广告、充斥电波的超级政治行动委员会。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I wanted to add this one last thing. We're going to talk about it more in the program. But this conversation around AIPAC and how it is dividing the Democratic Party and, to some extent, even Republicans. I remember Tom Massie's loss in Kentucky. He vehemently criticized AIPAC after.
paragraphs.cn: 我想补充最后一点。我们将在节目中进一步讨论。但围绕AIPAC以及它如何分裂民主党，甚至在某种程度上分裂共和党的对话。我记得托马斯·马西在肯塔基州的失利。他事后强烈批评了AIPAC。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Yes. And AIPAC has, in the last several years, become a real boogeyman in races. This obviously has a lot to do with politics involving Israel and Israel's shift --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。在过去的几年里，AIPAC在竞选中确实成了一个真正的恶棍。这显然与涉及以色列的政治以及以色列的转向有很大关系 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And exacerbated by October 7th and all that has followed since.
paragraphs.cn: 并且因10月7日事件及此后的一切而加剧。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Absolutely. And everything that has followed.
paragraphs.cn: 绝对如此。以及随后发生的一切。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: And it has created a political rift, both in the Democratic Party and in the Republican Party. And this backlash, you know, against Israel, which is a change.
paragraphs.cn: 这在民主党和共和党内部都造成了政治裂痕。这种对以色列的反弹是一种变化。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And that thread runs through Tucker Carlson's exit, as --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这条线索贯穿了塔克·卡尔森的离职 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: It runs all the way from Tucker Carlson to Zohran Mamdani.
paragraphs.cn: 从塔克·卡尔森一直延伸到佐兰·马姆达尼。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Exactly. So, we're going to talk more about that on the program today. We've got a lot to handle. I do want to bring up one other thing, coming up on CNN THIS MORNING. There's actually a new clue in the Nancy Guthrie case. What it could say about her disappearance. Plus, what's behind the tarp outside the Kennedy Center? New images showing what we now know is not there. And we're remembering the man with the golden ears who launched the careers of icons, including Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。所以，我们将在今天的节目中进一步讨论。我们有很多内容要处理。我还想提另一件事，即将在《CNN今晨》节目中播出。南希·格思里案中实际上出现了一条新线索。这可能说明她的失踪。此外，肯尼迪中心外的防水布背后是什么？新图像显示我们现在知道那里没有什么。我们还在纪念那位拥有金耳朵的人，他开启了包括惠特尼·休斯顿和布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀在内的偶像的职业生涯。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Legendary music producer Clive Davis has died at the age of 94. Here he is in 2024, talking about his legacy.
paragraphs.cn: 传奇音乐制作人克莱夫·戴维斯去世，享年94岁。这是他在2024年谈论自己遗产时的画面。

paragraphs.speaker: Clive Davis
paragraphs.en: I discovered or nurtured an unusual array of the most gifted artists of all time and that they felt safe.
paragraphs.cn: 我发掘或培养了有史以来最杰出的一批艺术家，他们感到安全。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It is now 12 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. Law enforcement sources telling CNN a note connected to the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie claims that she died, and that note was sent to media outlets back in February. We're told the kidnappers wrote it -- wrote that she died shortly after she was taken and that they did not mean to kill her. Now, this note followed one prior which demanded ransom. Investigators believe these notes are legitimate. And a major ruling on voting and privacy.
paragraphs.cn: 现在是整点过12分。这是你的早间新闻汇总。执法部门消息人士告诉CNN，与南希·格思里失踪有关的一张纸条声称她已经死亡，该纸条于今年二月被发送给媒体。我们被告知绑匪写道 -- -- 她被抓走后不久就死了，他们并非有意杀害她。这张纸条是在之前一张索要赎金的纸条之后出现的。调查人员认为这些纸条是真实的。还有一项关于投票和隐私的重大裁决。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The Trump administration blocked from using Social Security and immigration data to scrub voter rolls. A federal judge says the system relied on inaccurate information and risks wrongly removing eligible voters. Now, this ruling halted a key part of the president -- of the president's election overhaul plan. And we're getting a new look at what's behind the tarp at the Kennedy Center. These images, obtained by CNN, show President Trump's name is no longer on the building.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普政府被禁止使用社会保障和移民数据来清理选民名单。一名联邦法官表示，该系统依赖不准确的信息，并存在错误移除合格选民的风险。这项裁决暂停了总统选举改革计划的一个关键部分。我们还看到了肯尼迪中心幕布背后的新景象。CNN获得的这些图像显示，特朗普总统的名字已不在大楼上。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Workers began removing Trump's name from an exterior wall ten days ago, complying with a judge's ruling that the institution could only be named for John F. Kennedy as a living memorial to the late president. Ashley, I feel like you have some goss here. You're involved with the National Symphony?
paragraphs.cn: 工人们十天前开始从外墙移除特朗普的名字，以遵守法官的裁决，即该机构只能以约翰·F·肯尼迪的名字命名，作为对已故总统的活纪念碑。阿什利，我觉得你这里有些八卦。你与国家交响乐团有关联？

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Yes, absolutely. And I think it's really important, because the Kennedy Center -- also, the judge said the Kennedy Center will remain open for now, as well. And we'll see what happens there.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，当然。我认为这非常重要，因为肯尼迪中心 -- -- 而且法官表示肯尼迪中心目前将继续开放。我们拭目以待。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Instead of shutting down for a big Trump-induced renovation.
paragraphs.cn: 而不是因为特朗普引发的重大翻修而关闭。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Exactly. But the NSO is in to stay. And they are either going to do it with the symphony or some other way, if there's not going to be a budget to support them. But 100 percent, these musicians have stayed with the NSO from the beginning, and they --
paragraphs.cn: 没错。但国家交响乐团会留下来。他们要么与交响乐团合作，要么以其他方式，如果没有预算支持他们的话。但百分之百，这些音乐家从一开始就与国家交响乐团在一起，他们 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: None of them quit during --
paragraphs.cn: 没有人在这期间辞职 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- this big upheaval.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 在这场大动荡中。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: And we have a season to announce as soon as we can, and --
paragraphs.cn: 我们会尽快宣布新一季的演出安排，而且 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: National Symphony Orchestra.
paragraphs.cn: 国家交响乐团。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: That's why I don't know what the NSO is.
paragraphs.cn: 所以我才不知道NSO是什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Cornish
paragraphs.en: I'm going to take you to a concert. I'm going to take you to a concert.
paragraphs.cn: 我要带你去听一场音乐会。我要带你去听一场音乐会。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Shout-out to the National Symphony orchestra. Glad you guys stuck with it. Looking forward to the new season. I want to talk about this after the break. Does a quieter Fed equal a louder market? How Kevin Warsh's gamble might impact your money. Plus, the light at the end of the tunnel is here for Americans exposed on that hantavirus-stricken cruise ship. In the meantime, a live look in Houston. The World Cup play will continue there with a match featuring Portugal versus Uzbekistan.
paragraphs.cn: 向国家交响乐团致敬。很高兴你们坚持了下来。期待新一季的演出。我想在休息后讨论这个话题。美联储更安静是否意味着市场更喧嚣？凯文·沃什的赌注会如何影响你的钱袋子？此外，对于那艘爆发汉坦病毒的游轮上暴露的美国人来说，隧道尽头的光明已经出现。与此同时，休斯顿的现场画面。世界杯比赛将继续在那里进行，葡萄牙对阵乌兹别克斯坦。

paragraphs.speaker: Kevin Warsh
paragraphs.en: I don't share the view that was expressed a few generations ago that Federal Reserve chairmen show up at a podium like this and say, you've got to choose, and you're going to have to decide whether you're willing to tolerate higher inflation to put more people at work.
paragraphs.cn: 我不认同几代人之前表达的观点，即美联储主席站在这样的讲台上说，你必须做出选择，你必须决定是否愿意容忍更高的通胀以让更多人就业。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Newly sworn-in Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, leaving interest rates steady for now. But concerning economic data, since the start of the war with Iran could mean he'll have to change his mind. So let me give you an example. Inflation rose half a percent from April to May. That means it's now around 4.2 percent. Consumer confidence dipping among Americans. So, joining me now Courtenay Brown. She's the senior economics reporter at Axios. Welcome back. Thanks so much.
paragraphs.cn: 新任美联储主席凯文·沃什宣誓就职，暂时维持利率不变。但自与伊朗开战以来的经济数据可能意味着他不得不改变主意。举个例子，通胀率从4月到5月上升了0.5个百分点，目前约为4.2%。美国消费者信心下降。现在请来Axios高级经济记者考特尼·布朗。欢迎回来，非常感谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, I'm starting with the inflation, just to give people a sense, because that's always the boogeyman. Is inflation high? Is inflation low? And this is where monetary policy comes into play. What is our sense, now that Warsh is in the job, of how he thinks about these things?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，我先从通胀说起，让大家有个概念，因为通胀总是那个吓人的东西。通胀高吗？通胀低吗？这就是货币政策发挥作用的地方。现在沃什上任了，我们对他如何看待这些问题有什么了解？

paragraphs.speaker: Courtenay Brown
paragraphs.en: Do you want to hear something crazy?
paragraphs.cn: 你想听点疯狂的事吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: About Fed policy, Courtenay. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 关于美联储政策，考特尼。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: Because he is not so -- Warsh's big audition was last week. First Fed policy meeting. We get the closely parsed policy statement. It is 130 words, more than a third of the length in the Powell era, just months before. He wants to say less. He wants to guide markets less. What we know is that he said that inflation -- or the Fed will accomplish price stability. A wonky way of saying they will get inflation back to 2 percent. As you said, so far from 2 percent right now.
paragraphs.cn: 因为他并不那么 -- -- 沃什的大考在上周。首次美联储政策会议。我们得到了仔细解读的政策声明。它只有130个单词，比鲍威尔时代篇幅的三分之一还多，就在几个月前。他想说得更少。他想减少对市场的引导。我们知道的是，他说通胀 -- -- 或者说美联储将实现物价稳定。这是一种迂回的说法，意思是他们会把通胀率拉回2%。正如你所说，现在离2%还差得远。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Can I -- let's look at the chart. She was making fun of me trying to read my own charts here for business.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我能 -- -- 我们来看看这张图表。她刚才在嘲笑我试图在这里读我自己的商业图表。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Consumer price index.
paragraphs.cn: 消费者价格指数。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. What are we looking at in this chart right now?
paragraphs.cn: 好的。我们现在在这张图表上看到的是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: So, what we've seen is inflation shooting right up since the war.
paragraphs.cn: 我们看到的是，自战争以来通胀率急剧上升。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, see, I did the same thing guys. That's confidence. Give me consumer price.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，看，我也做了同样的事，伙计们。这就是信心。给我消费者价格。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: But that's an important story, too.
paragraphs.cn: 但这也是一个重要的话题。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: The stories are connected.
paragraphs.cn: 这些话题是相互关联的。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: Because inflation is shooting up, consumers feel really crappy about the economy right now. We all know this. Not a new story. It is a -- it is partially because of inflation, but it's also because of price levels. Prices have just been really high and have not come down. So, I think what the Fed has to grapple with here is how do you bring inflation down without wrecking the economy? Financial markets have decided that the Fed will probably raise interest rates this year. That's crazy.
paragraphs.cn: 因为通胀率急剧上升，消费者现在对经济感觉非常糟糕。我们都知道这一点。这不是什么新鲜事。部分原因是通胀，但也因为价格水平。价格一直很高，而且没有降下来。所以，我认为美联储必须应对的问题是，如何在不让经济崩溃的情况下降低通胀？金融市场已经认定美联储今年可能会加息。这太疯狂了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: "Wall Street Journal" saying Fed could raise -- could raise it three times this year.
paragraphs.cn: 《华尔街日报》称美联储今年可能加息三次。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: Everyone on Wall Street --
paragraphs.cn: 华尔街的每个人 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- reporting from the forecast from Bank of America.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 都在报道美国银行的预测。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: Everyone on Wall Street is revising their expectations for rate hikes this year. When just a few months ago, they were expecting rate cuts. But we don't actually know. Kevin Warsh didn't say that. He doesn't want to guide markets in the way that traditional Fed chairman have. He wants to go back to the Greenspan era, the late Alan Greenspan, who died yesterday.
paragraphs.cn: 华尔街的每个人都在修正他们对今年加息的预期。而就在几个月前，他们还预期会降息。但我们实际上并不知道。凯文·沃什没有这么说。他不想像传统美联储主席那样引导市场。他想回到格林斯潘时代，即已故的艾伦·格林斯潘，他昨天去世了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let's take a beat on that.
paragraphs.cn: 让我们先缓一缓。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Because there's some familiar language here. When the -- the late Alan Greenspan, who died this week, when he took over the Fed from Paul Volcker, you had Gulf wars fueling spikes in prices, right? So, something going on in the Middle East. You had high inflation, and you had real worries about that and prices. So, some of this feels a bit familiar for us in this moment under Trump's term. What did Greenspan do that's part of his legacy, why people still think of him with -- with such reverence?
paragraphs.cn: 因为这里有一些熟悉的说法。当已故的艾伦·格林斯潘（本周去世）从保罗·沃尔克手中接管美联储时，海湾战争导致价格飙升，对吧？所以，中东发生了一些事情。当时通胀高企，人们对此和物价感到切实担忧。所以，在特朗普任期的此刻，我们感觉有些似曾相识。格林斯潘做了什么，成为他遗产的一部分，为什么人们至今仍对他如此崇敬？

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: The celebrity Fed chairman of our era, really. There's one more parallel to this moment that's quite spooky. Alan Greenspan, during his term, he presided -- or he led the Fed over a period where there was huge technological change, the Internet boom of the '90s.
paragraphs.cn: 他真是我们这个时代的名人美联储主席。还有一个与此刻非常诡异的相似之处。艾伦·格林斯潘在任期间，他主持 -- -- 或者说领导美联储度过了一个技术巨变的时期，即90年代的互联网繁荣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, yes. And its bubble.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，是的。还有它的泡沫。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: Yes. Sound like A.I. a little bit right now?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。听起来有点像现在的人工智能？

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: Maybe. So, I think Alan Greenspan is having such a moment right now. It is so interesting to me that he died at the -- at the moment where his legacy is kind of front and center --
paragraphs.cn: 也许吧。所以，我认为艾伦·格林斯潘此刻正备受关注。我觉得非常有趣的是，他去世的时刻，他的遗产正好处于焦点位置 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: -- among Trump economic officials. You hear them shouting out Alan Greenspan because of what he did. He -- he believed that, then the Internet boom was going to usher in this huge era of productivity. And it would not be inflationary. And we've heard the White House explicitly say that they think the Fed can do the very same thing in this moment: keep interest rates low, not cut off this economic boom that we're having.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 在特朗普的经济官员中。你听到他们大声赞扬艾伦·格林斯潘，因为他所做的事。他相信互联网繁荣将带来一个巨大的生产力时代，而且不会引发通胀。我们听到白宫明确表示，他们认为美联储此刻可以做同样的事情：保持低利率，不切断我们正在经历的经济繁荣。

paragraphs.speaker: Brown
paragraphs.en: But the problem is exactly what you say. We have this inflation issue. It's really hard for the Fed not to raise interest rates in the face of inflation that is soaring now, but was already sticky before the war. So, they have a problem.
paragraphs.cn: 但问题正如你所说。我们面临通胀问题。面对目前飙升的通胀 -- -- 尽管在战争前就已经居高不下 -- -- 美联储很难不加息。所以，他们面临一个难题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: One other thing I want to read from Alan Greenspan, because I think it's relevant here. In 1994, he said, "We will be at price stability when households and businesses need not factor expectations of changes in the average level of prices into their decisions." I'm going to translate and say he cared about our prices, about how households were able to afford their economy.
paragraphs.cn: 我还想引用艾伦·格林斯潘的一句话，因为我认为这与此相关。1994年，他说："当家庭和企业无需将平均价格水平变化的预期纳入决策时，我们就实现了价格稳定。"我翻译一下，意思是他关心我们的物价，关心家庭如何负担得起他们的经济生活。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Courtenay, thank you so much. Love having you. I'm going to come up with more excuses to cover Fed policy just to soak in your enthusiasm. Next on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to talk about the issues from transgender athletes to birthright citizenship before the Supreme Court. We could get some opinions later today, before its summer recess. Plus, mass firings underway at the nation's top intel office. And lawmakers warn of serious risks with each lost job. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 考特尼，非常感谢你。很高兴你能来。我会找更多借口来报道美联储政策，只为沉浸在你的热情中。接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，我们将讨论最高法院审理的从跨性别运动员到出生公民权等问题。今天晚些时候，在最高法院夏季休庭前，我们可能会得到一些意见。此外，国家最高情报机构正在进行大规模裁员。议员们警告说，每失去一个工作岗位都伴随着严重风险。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. Thank you for joining me on CNN THIS MORNING. It is half past the hour. And here's what's happening right now. Secretary of State Marco Rubio heads to the Middle East. He is the first White House official to do so after the signing of the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding. Rubio will be in the region discussing that deal, along with, quote, regional priorities, including the Strait of Hormuz.
paragraphs.cn: 大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。感谢您收看《CNN今晨》节目。现在是半点钟。以下是正在发生的事情。国务卿马可·卢比奥前往中东。他是美伊谅解备忘录签署后首位前往该地区的白宫官员。卢比奥将在该地区讨论该协议，以及所谓的"地区优先事项"，包括霍尔木兹海峡。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: And quarantine finally over for the last eight American passengers exposed to hantavirus who were staying at a Nebraska facility. Now, they were exposed to that virus on a cruise ship early last month. The passengers stayed in the quarantine unit for 42 days. And Messi makes history again. He scored two goals in Argentina's win over Austria, officially earning him the most goals scored in World Cup history, 18.
paragraphs.cn: 最后八名接触过汉坦病毒的美国乘客终于结束了隔离，他们一直住在内布拉斯加州的一家设施内。这些乘客上月初在一艘游轮上接触了该病毒。他们在隔离区待了42天。梅西再次创造历史。他在阿根廷战胜奥地利的比赛中攻入两球，正式成为世界杯历史进球最多的球员，共18球。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Messi. Messi's a beast. Messi came to get the record of most goals in the World Cup, and he got it. He got two of them.
paragraphs.cn: 梅西。梅西是个猛兽。梅西来打破世界杯进球纪录，他做到了。他进了两个球。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But Messi will be fighting to keep the record. France, on the right, on his heels with 16 all-time goals. And this morning the Supreme Court expected to release some opinions for its outstanding docket. So, you've got cases still left outstanding, those include birthright citizenship. Also, presidential firing authority and transgender athletes. These are all opinions the Trump administration has a vested interest in.
paragraphs.cn: 但梅西将努力保持这一纪录。法国队以16个总进球紧随其后。今天上午，最高法院预计将就其未决案件发布一些意见。目前仍有未决案件，包括出生公民权、总统解雇权以及跨性别运动员。特朗普政府对这些意见都有切身利益。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And Trump has already lashed out against the Supreme Court over decisions relating to, say, his tariff policy. So, could these opinions further erode the relationship between the executive and judiciary? The group chat is back. The big one here I want to mention is birthright citizenship. This could really be something that would annoy Trump. And, honestly, it's not looking that good because it's one of those things where it's kind of in the Constitution.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普已经就关税政策等相关裁决抨击过最高法院。那么，这些意见是否会进一步损害行政与司法部门之间的关系？群聊又回来了。我想提的一个重要问题是出生公民权。这可能会让特朗普感到恼火。老实说，情况看起来不太乐观，因为这是宪法中有所规定的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: What are you thinking about how he's feeling? TAMARA KEITH, HOST, "NPR POLITICS PODCAST": Yes, it was one of his day one priorities in his second term. It's something that he campaigned on. It's also something that legal experts have been extremely dubious about the entire time.
paragraphs.cn: 你觉得他现在感觉如何？塔玛拉·基思（《NPR政治播客》主持人）：是的，这是他第二任期首日的优先事项之一。这是他竞选时承诺的。同时，法律专家一直对此深表怀疑。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: And I do think that we could expect him, if it goes against him, to be very unhappy and not to be quiet about that because he is never quiet when he is unhappy. And you saw that with the tariff ruling. And, you know, like the relationship between --
paragraphs.cn: 我确实认为，如果裁决对他不利，我们可以预期他会非常不满，并且不会保持沉默，因为他从不掩饰自己的不满。你在关税裁决中已经看到了这一点。而且，你知道，就像行政与司法之间的关系 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: I mean, like even talking about it as a relationship anymore --
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，甚至再把它当作一段关系来谈论 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But even many of these things, out of all the -- everything on the list, I feel like the transgender athlete case is the one that becomes a talking point in elections, right? In this case, it's two students challenging state laws, banning participation in -- their participation in girl's sports, arguing that it violates Title IX. Whereas like, the abortion ruling, so many years ago, became this, like, pivotal thing for Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: 但即使是这些事情中的许多，在清单上的所有事情中，我觉得跨性别运动员案件会成为选举中的一个话题，对吧？在这个案件中，两名学生挑战州法律，禁止她们参加女子体育项目，认为这违反了《教育法修正案第九条》。而多年前的堕胎裁决，则成了民主党人的一个关键议题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Out of everything here, mail-in ballots, birthright citizenship, it feels like you're going to see ads about a transgender athlete ruling.
paragraphs.cn: 在所有这些事情中，邮寄选票、出生公民权，感觉你会看到关于跨性别运动员裁决的广告。

paragraphs.speaker: Ashley Davis
paragraphs.en: It only becomes an issue if they say that you can have the transgender athletes in the sports. I think that if they say, no, you can't, I don't know, are Democrats going to defend that?
paragraphs.cn: 只有当他们说你可以让跨性别运动员参加体育比赛时，这才成为一个问题。我认为如果他们说不行，我不知道，民主党人会为此辩护吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Chuck Rocha
paragraphs.en: I think that you're already seeing Republicans use transgender, whether or not the Supreme Court does this or not, you're going to see two types of ads, and we're making hundreds of ads right now in these congressional seats. And you have Democrats talking about cost of living, and you have Republicans talking about trans athletes or somebody having low t. That's going to be the argument of -- we're seeing what the midterms --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为你已经看到共和党人在利用跨性别议题，无论最高法院是否这样做，你都会看到两种类型的广告，我们现在正在这些国会选区制作数百个广告。民主党人在谈论生活成本，而共和党人在谈论跨性别运动员或某人睾酮水平低。这将成为争论的焦点 -- -- 我们正在看到中期选举的情况 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Low t. The low t, testosterone, conversation.
paragraphs.cn: 睾酮水平低。关于低睾酮的讨论。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: I like that. Do you like that?
paragraphs.cn: 我喜欢这个。你喜欢吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Why is it in politics?
paragraphs.cn: 为什么它会出现在政治中？

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: I'm just telling you what they got.
paragraphs.cn: 我只是告诉你他们有什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's like, guys, just save it for your supplements. I don't need --
paragraphs.cn: 就像，伙计们，把它留给你们的补品吧。我不需要 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: If you ain't got nothing else. (CROSS TALK).
paragraphs.cn: 如果你没有别的了。（交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: They're talking about low t?
paragraphs.cn: 他们在说低睾酮吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: I'm just saying, as a boy who takes a lot of protein, this is what they're doing and it's going to lose momentum (ph).
paragraphs.cn: 我只是说，作为一个摄入大量蛋白质的男孩，他们正在这样做，但这会失去动力。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's what they're calling Talarico. That's what they're calling Talarico. Which I get, but just the whole --
paragraphs.cn: 他们就是这么称呼塔拉里科的。他们就是这么称呼塔拉里科的。我明白，但整个 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: But it worked really well last time. I don't know if it's still the same.
paragraphs.cn: 但上次效果很好。我不知道现在是否还是一样。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: It's a different time.
paragraphs.cn: 时代不同了。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Unless there's a bad ruling or a --
paragraphs.cn: 除非有一个糟糕的裁决或 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: People are sick and tired right now. They're sick and tired of these prices. I just went home to see my mama this weekend for her 74th birthday, and only anything could say is that folks can't afford stuff.
paragraphs.cn: 人们现在厌倦了。他们厌倦了这些价格。这个周末我回家看我妈妈过她74岁生日，唯一能说的是人们买不起东西。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But, Chuck, isn't the argument that Democrats, too, are not focusing on the right things because they are focused on their affinity, communities or not eating steak.
paragraphs.cn: 但是，查克，难道不是有人认为民主党人也没有关注正确的事情，因为他们关注的是他们的亲和力、社区或者不吃牛排吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Smart people like you want to talk about focus.
paragraphs.cn: 像你这样的聪明人想谈论焦点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No, no, what I'm saying is that's --
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，我的意思是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: I'm talking about ads.
paragraphs.cn: 我在说广告。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, don't ads generate focus? You -- they're determining where to look.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，广告难道不产生焦点吗？你 -- -- 它们决定了人们看哪里。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And I think Republicans are going to say, don't look over here where they're talking about affordability, look over here.
paragraphs.cn: 而且我认为共和党人会这么说：别看向这边，他们在谈论负担能力，看向这边。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: You're right. That's right.
paragraphs.cn: 你说得对。没错。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: But that's what they're running against Talarico with your buddy on is they're going to go over some of these issues --
paragraphs.cn: 但这就是他们和你的朋友一起对抗Talarico的方式 -- -- 他们会谈论其中一些问题 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Because they can't talk about what Audie just said, which is the cost of things.
paragraphs.cn: 因为他们不能谈论Audie刚才说的，也就是物价。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: You can't talk about that. But you talk about something else.
paragraphs.cn: 你不能谈那个。但你可以谈别的。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Some of those ads are effective, I'm sorry to say.
paragraphs.cn: 有些广告是有效的，我很遗憾地说。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Some of them with the base.
paragraphs.cn: 有些是针对基本盘的。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Well, one other thing I want to add is, there is this idea, a Senate committee advancing a bipartisan bill to allow oral arguments to be televised, saying that this would be good for transparency. "The Washington Post" editorial board joining them. Or, sorry, "The Washington Post" editorial board complaining that this will contribute to grandstanding. You're laughing. I mean, I have to admit, I'm good with audio.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我还想补充一点，有一种观点认为，参议院一个委员会正在推进一项两党法案，允许电视转播口头辩论，称这将有利于透明度。《华盛顿邮报》的编委会也加入其中。或者，抱歉，《华盛顿邮报》的编委会抱怨说，这会导致作秀。你在笑。我的意思是，我得承认，我赞成音频。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: I -- yes, I'm, as a (INAUDIBLE), I'm thrilled with the audio.
paragraphs.cn: 我 -- -- 是的，作为（听不清），我对音频感到兴奋。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: As audio people, we're happy already.
paragraphs.cn: 作为音频人，我们已经很高兴了。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Very happy already. I don't know that you can grandstand in the Supreme Court because I don't think the justices will stand for that. They are asking deep dive questions about very specific things.
paragraphs.cn: 已经非常高兴了。我不知道在最高法院能否作秀，因为我认为大法官们不会容忍。他们会就非常具体的问题提出深入的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But I think what they can -- what they say can be clipped and used against people in election slop. Like, I don't think --
paragraphs.cn: 但我认为他们所说的话可能会被剪辑，并在选举中被用来攻击别人。比如，我不认为 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Yes, but they're not going to bother.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但他们不会费心。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: People are going to use those same televised clips to make a thing.
paragraphs.cn: 人们会利用那些同样的电视剪辑片段来制造事端。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: And we'll use them. We'll use an audio ad overlay pictures all day long in a TV ad.
paragraphs.cn: 我们会利用它们。我们会在电视广告中整天使用音频叠加图片。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Especially if they -- yes.
paragraphs.cn: 特别是如果他们 -- -- 是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, I want to turn to something else that's just a little bit more important, especially since we're going to have those rulings later today. There is a source telling CNN that the so-called, quote, deep state firings have begun at the Office of the Director of National Intelligence. Here's why we're watching this. President Trump's new acting spy chief, Bill Pult, he's actually in charge already, right? Not -- this hasn't been confirmed or anything like that. He's interim.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，我想转向另一个更重要的事情，特别是因为我们今天晚些时候会有那些裁决。有消息人士告诉CNN，所谓的"深层政府"解雇行动已经在国家情报总监办公室开始。我们关注此事的原因是，特朗普总统的新任代理情报总监比尔·普尔特实际上已经上任了，对吧？这不是 -- -- 这还没有得到确认或类似的东西。他是临时的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And Democrats are raising concerns. SEN. CHRIS VAN HOLLEN (D-MD): Bill Pulte is somebody who has zero, zero experience when it comes to national security and intelligence matters. His sort of defining mission in the Trump administration has been as a political hit man for Donald Trump to go after his political adversaries. And now this is the guy who's going to be firing people with experience and expertise when it comes to our national security intelligence matters. So, what could go wrong?
paragraphs.cn: 而民主党人正表达担忧。参议员克里斯·范·霍伦（马里兰州民主党人）表示：比尔·普尔特在国家安全和情报事务方面毫无经验。他在特朗普政府中的主要使命就是充当唐纳德·特朗普的政治打手，去打击他的政治对手。而现在，这个人将负责解雇那些在我们国家安全情报事务方面有经验和专业知识的人。那么，这能不出问题吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, so here's the thing. I'm not one for the 3-D chess argument. But this is the person Trump wants. Bill Pulte, who comes from housing policy, that's not the person that anyone in the Senate seems to want.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，事情是这样的。我并不是那种认为这是三步棋的人。但这就是特朗普想要的人。比尔·普尔特出身于住房政策领域，这似乎并不是参议院中任何人想要的人选。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Including Republicans.
paragraphs.cn: 包括共和党人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Including Republicans. Let's play an example. Lindsey Graham, this Sunday on CBS, complaining that the president wasn't letting Jay Clayton, the person they actually feel like has the votes, testify. SEN. LINDSEY GRAHAM (R-SC): And I would urge President Trump to let Clayton testify. And if we nominate Clayton and get him affirmed as the new DNI, we don't want FISA to go dark. There's too many threats to our country for 702 to go down.
paragraphs.cn: 包括共和党人。我们来看一个例子。林赛·格雷厄姆本周日在哥伦比亚广播公司节目中抱怨说，总统不让杰伊·克莱顿作证，而克莱顿是他们认为确实有票数通过的人。参议员林赛·格雷厄姆（南卡罗来纳州共和党人）表示：我会敦促特朗普总统让克莱顿作证。如果我们提名克莱顿并让他确认为新的国家情报总监，我们不想让《外国情报监视法》失效。702条款如果失效，我们的国家面临太多威胁。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Anybody who owns the shutting down of 702 under FISA will own a future attack against the United States.
paragraphs.cn: 任何导致《外国情报监视法》下702条款失效的人，都将对未来的美国遭受攻击负责。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, there is this FISA surveillance law that allows the government to go after intelligence targets abroad and inadvertently maybe capture some U.S. information. The point is, his need to have Pulte looks like it's starting to hold up other aspects of the administration's agenda.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这项《外国情报监视法》允许政府追踪海外情报目标，并可能无意中捕获一些美国信息。关键是，他需要普尔特这一点似乎开始阻碍政府议程的其他方面。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Yes, it is. But President Trump has been very focused for a very long time about retribution, whatever you want to call it. He feels like the intelligence community went after him in 2016. Russia, Russia, Russia. He feels like his first impeachment was the result of the intelligence community. And Bill Pulte is there to do a job.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，确实如此。但特朗普总统长期以来一直非常关注报复，无论你怎么称呼它。他觉得情报界在2016年针对了他。俄罗斯、俄罗斯、俄罗斯。他觉得他的第一次弹劾是情报界的结果。而比尔·普尔特就是来做这项工作的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I think what's shady to me is that --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为对我来说可疑的是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Like to get rid of these --
paragraphs.cn: 比如要除掉这些人 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Of course. But he's interim. Like, he has Clayton right there. He could put someone forth that Republicans are willing to give him the votes for. And instead, just to give the reviews here, here is what some of these Republicans have been saying over the last couple of weeks when Pulte's name was raised. SEN. THOM TILLIS (R-NC): I think he's the worst form of sycophant and advisor to the president that is going to hurt the president's legacy. SEN.
paragraphs.cn: 当然。但他只是临时人选。比如，他手边就有克莱顿。他可以推举一个共和党人愿意投票支持的人。然而，为了回顾一下，以下是过去几周当普尔特的名字被提及时，一些共和党人所说的话。参议员汤姆·蒂利斯（北卡罗来纳州共和党人）表示：我认为他是最糟糕的谄媚者和总统顾问，这将损害总统的遗产。参议员

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: JOHN CORNYN (R-TX): I see no, no evidence of any qualifications for that job. SEN. KEVIN CRAMER (R-ND): I don't know Jay Clayton, but I was quite certain the acting guy wasn't going to cut it, shall we say, Pulte? SEN. JOHN KENNEDY (R-LA): What's always worried me about Mr. Pulte, he doesn't have a real deep background in intelligence.
paragraphs.cn: 约翰·科宁（德克萨斯州共和党参议员）：我看不出有任何证据表明他具备担任该职务的资格。凯文·克莱默（北达科他州共和党参议员）：我不认识杰伊·克莱顿，但我很确定那个代理的人干不了这活，我们这么说吧，普尔特？约翰·肯尼迪（路易斯安那州共和党参议员）：我一直担心普尔特先生的是，他在情报领域没有真正深厚的背景。

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.en: And Pulte, Pulte's (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: 而普尔特，普尔特（听不清）。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I have no observations on the matter.
paragraphs.cn: 我对此事没有看法。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I'm going to start using that one, actually. No, keep in mind, this person --
paragraphs.cn: 我其实打算开始用那个说法了。不，记住，这个人 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Actually, they (ph) just laugh after he said that. Did you see that?
paragraphs.cn: 实际上，他说完那句话后他们（口误）就笑了。你看到了吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That the person they're describing is now already firing people.
paragraphs.cn: 他们描述的那个人现在已经在解雇人了。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Well, I'm OK with actually -- like taking everything else out of it. There's so many things here. First of all, I'm hoping that Clayton issue gets solved tomorrow when the president goes up and talks to Senate Republicans because I actually think that is part of a broader issue. Pulte is in there for one thing, as everyone said, which is to get rid of a lot of these people. I was around when we created this agency, the DNI.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我其实可以接受 -- -- 比如把其他所有东西都抛开。这里有很多事情。首先，我希望克莱顿的问题明天能解决，总统去和参议院共和党人谈话时，因为我实际上认为这是一个更广泛问题的一部分。普尔特在那里做一件事，正如所有人所说，就是除掉很多人。我们创建这个机构 -- -- 国家情报总监办公室时，我就在场。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: I think that there's a lot of issues with it, and there's a lot of fat, but there's also a lot of leaks. The problem is, is Bill is a little bit -- not a little bit. Bill is a lightning rod because he doesn't have any internet (ph). He doesn't have any background in intelligence.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为它有很多问题，有很多冗余，但也有很多泄密。问题是，比尔有点 -- -- 不是有点。比尔是个避雷针，因为他没有任何网络（口误）。他没有任何情报背景。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Is that the only reason it's a lightning rod, Ashley?
paragraphs.cn: 这是他是避雷针的唯一原因吗，阿什莉？

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Well, no. He's also a lightning rod just because of all of his policies. I mean he actually did a lot of cage rattling within the -- in the West Wing. So, there is -- there's not a big -- there's not a -- he has a lot of enemies and a lot of allies, but his number one -- the number --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，不。他也是个避雷针，仅仅因为他的所有政策。我的意思是，他在白宫西翼内部确实引起了很多震动。所以，他有很多敌人和很多盟友，但他的首要 -- -- 首要 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Didn't somebody already hit him at the White House? Didn't somebody punch him in the face?
paragraphs.cn: 不是已经有人在白宫揍过他了吗？没人朝他脸上打一拳吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: No, that wasn't -- that was -- that was Secretary Bessent.
paragraphs.cn: 不，那不是 -- -- 那是 -- -- 那是贝森特部长。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: And he just threatened to.
paragraphs.cn: 而他只是威胁要这么做。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, just threatened. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 对，只是威胁。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: I get my MMA mixed up all the time at the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 我在白宫总是把综合格斗搞混。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: But just -- I just want to say something, like, yes, that's true.
paragraphs.cn: 但我想说的是，没错，确实如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: There is something that's really important. The DNI agency is the most leaked agency in the intelligence world. There needs to be changes there. It's just, the controversy is around the person that's doing it.
paragraphs.cn: 有一件事非常重要。国家情报总监办公室是情报界泄密最多的机构。那里需要改变。只是争议在于执行这件事的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Should a person who doesn't have the job yet, is not confirmed by the Senate, be firing people already? That seems like it opens the door for people --
paragraphs.cn: 一个还没上任、未经参议院确认的人，应该已经开始解雇人了吗？这似乎为人们打开了 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: If they're leaking stuff, I don't care who fires them.
paragraphs.cn: 如果他们泄露东西，我才不管是谁解雇他们。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But we don't know why. That's the thing.
paragraphs.cn: 但我们不知道原因。问题就在这里。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Well, there's major investigations going on that are classified of who's leaking. So, yes, I think that if -- those people should be fired if they're leaking national --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，目前正在进行一些重大的机密调查，关于谁在泄密。所以，是的，我认为如果 -- -- 那些泄露国家机密的人应该被解雇。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: I think Trump's going to get exactly what he wants. He wants this.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为特朗普会得到他想要的东西。他想要这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Trump wants Pulte firing everybody. And then he wants to say, OK, we got rid of that bad guy. We'll bring Clayton in. Everybody likes this Clayton guy because they put up the worst of the worst of somebody that's totally not qualified to do this job.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普想让普尔特解雇所有人。然后他想说，好吧，我们摆脱了那个坏人。我们会让克莱顿进来。大家都喜欢这个克莱顿，因为他们把最糟糕、最不合格的人推出来做这份工作。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It seems like the recipe for a lawsuit. But, like, I'm not a lawyer. I don't know.
paragraphs.cn: 这看起来像是会引发诉讼。但是，我不是律师。我不知道。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: But I think -- well, I'm -- I mean, that's like the everyone is --
paragraphs.cn: 但我认为 -- -- 嗯，我是说，这就像每个人都在 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Do you know what I mean? Like, it kind of feels like, are you fired without cause? Like, can you raise the question? (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: 你明白我的意思吗？感觉像是，你是无故被解雇的吗？你能提出这个问题吗？（交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: I like to -- I'm making my prediction. I think Clayton's confirmed in July.
paragraphs.cn: 我想 -- -- 我在做预测。我认为克莱顿会在七月得到确认。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I think he's -- you're both right. He's confirmed. But after a bunch of firings.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为他 -- -- 你们俩都对。他会得到确认。但在一系列解雇之后。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: No, in July. I think he gets confirmed in July.
paragraphs.cn: 不，在七月。我认为他会在七月得到确认。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: (INAUDIBLE). Tell her (INAUDIBLE). Here we go.
paragraphs.cn: （听不清）。告诉她（听不清）。开始吧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, you guys, there's one more. I want to talk about this. This is actually really interesting. Next on CNN THIS MORNING, Tucker Carlson claiming he's out of the Republican Party. This is years after defending Trump to the MAGA movement. So, we're wondering where he's going to take his support next. And then later on CNN, a White House plot grows. There are new arrests revealing drones, explosives and a widening network connected with the alleged plot to attack the UFC event.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，各位，还有一件事。我想谈谈这个。这其实非常有趣。接下来在CNN今晨节目中，塔克·卡尔森声称他退出了共和党。这距离他曾在MAGA运动中为特朗普辩护已有数年。所以，我们想知道他接下来会把支持投向哪里。随后在CNN上，一起白宫阴谋逐渐浮出水面。新的逮捕行动揭示了无人机、爆炸物以及与涉嫌袭击UFC赛事阴谋相关的不断扩大的网络。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, the Democratic Party, as we talked about, continues to struggle with its messaging on Israel in the wake of the '24 election. Today, as voters in New York head to the polls, a pro-Israeli lobbying group highlights a rift in the party. AIPAC, American Israel Public Affairs Committee, it's donated nearly $42 million to campaigns this year alone. And several high-profile Democrats, Cory Booker, Gavin Newsom, AOC, say they refuse to accept AIPAC funds.
paragraphs.cn: 正如我们之前讨论的，民主党在24年大选后仍在以色列问题上的信息传递中挣扎。今天，随着纽约选民前往投票站，一个亲以色列游说团体凸显了党内的分歧。AIPAC，即美国以色列公共事务委员会，仅今年就向竞选活动捐赠了近4200万美元。而几位知名民主党人，科里·布克、加文·纽森、AOC，表示他们拒绝接受AIPAC的资金。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: In New York, Mayor Zohran Mamdani also calling out candidates who do not take that money -- candidates who do take that money. MAYOR ZOHRAN MAMDANI (D),
paragraphs.cn: 在纽约，市长佐兰·马姆达尼也指责那些接受这笔资金的候选人 -- -- 以及那些不接受这笔资金的候选人。市长佐兰·马姆达尼（民主党）：

paragraphs.speaker: New York City
paragraphs.en: The old world is dying and the new world struggles to be born. Now is the time of monsters. These monsters take many forms today. In AIPAC, for whom the only thing more frightening than democracy being allowed to run its course is an end to genocide in Netanyahu's wars. They move millions in dark money to accomplish a single goal, to preserve their power so that they can turn us against one another, instead of our leaders turning towards the moral change we all know to be necessary.
paragraphs.cn: 旧世界正在消亡，新世界挣扎着诞生。现在是怪物的时代。这些怪物今天以多种形式出现。在AIPAC，对他们来说，唯一比允许民主自然发展更可怕的事情，就是结束内塔尼亚胡战争中的种族灭绝。他们动用数百万黑钱来实现一个目标：维护他们的权力，以便他们能让我们彼此对立，而不是让我们的领导人转向我们都知道必要的道德变革。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. He was specifically pointing to Congressman Adriano Espaillat, who is running against one of Mamdani's backed candidates. Now, according to the Federal Election Commission, AIPAC made two donations last month totaling around $600,000. Ted Deutch, the CEO of the American Jewish Committee, called Mamdani's comments, quote, "outrageous and dangerous, and the impact of your words extend far beyond politics." Mamdani has doubled down. MAYOR ZOHRAN MAMDANI (D),
paragraphs.cn: 好的。他特别指向了国会议员阿德里亚诺·埃斯帕亚特，后者正在与马姆达尼支持的一名候选人竞争。根据联邦选举委员会的数据，AIPAC上个月进行了两笔捐款，总额约60万美元。美国犹太人委员会首席执行官泰德·多伊奇称马姆达尼的言论"令人愤慨且危险，你的话的影响远远超出了政治范畴。"马姆达尼则加倍坚持。市长佐兰·马姆达尼（民主党）：

paragraphs.speaker: New York
paragraphs.en: I want to be very clear. We're talking about a status quo where children are being killed on a daily basis. And when I am speaking about AIPAC, I'm speaking about an organization that has been supportive of the status quo, that has fought any attempt to actually deliver safety to people, not just in Palestine, but, frankly, through much of the region. And it is a status quo for immorality. It is one that I will not accept.
paragraphs.cn: 我想说得很清楚。我们谈论的是一种儿童每天被杀害的现状。当我谈到AIPAC时，我指的是一个支持这种现状的组织，它反对任何真正为人们带来安全的尝试，不仅在巴勒斯坦，坦率地说，在整个地区大部分地方都是如此。这是一种不道德的现状。我不会接受它。

paragraphs.speaker: New York
paragraphs.en: And I think that it is important that when we ask ourselves how such death and destruction is happening overseas, we also name those who allow it to take place.
paragraphs.cn: 而且我认为，当我们问自己海外为何发生如此多的死亡和破坏时，我们也应该点名那些允许这一切发生的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining me now, Errol Louis, political anchor with Spectrum News. Errol, in a minute I want to play for you Rep, Dan Goldman, who had kind of an altercation with a coffee shop around this issue of anti- Semitism. But first, can you talk about how this AIPAC conversation, why it's playing such a big role in these essentially small city races?
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入我的是厄尔·路易斯，Spectrum新闻的政治主播。厄尔，稍后我想为你播放众议员丹·戈德曼的片段，他曾在反犹太主义问题上与一家咖啡店发生争执。但首先，你能谈谈这场关于AIPAC的对话，为什么它在这些基本上是小型城市竞选中扮演如此重要的角色吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Errol Louis
paragraphs.en: Yes. Good morning, Audie. The issue of AIPAC is really shorthand for opposing Israel's policies in Gaza and elsewhere in the region. And so, they've done what politicians do with slogans. They've made it so that it basically ends the conversation just to say, hey, you took money from AIPAC and then you can't say much more about it. And that's, you know, this is -- this is what happens in the middle of a heated campaign.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。早上好，奥迪。AIPAC问题实际上是反对以色列在加沙及该地区其他地区政策的简写。所以，他们做了政客们用口号做的事。他们让这句话基本上结束了对话：嘿，你从AIPAC拿了钱，然后你就不能再多说别的了。你知道，这就是 -- -- 这就是激烈竞选期间会发生的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Errol Louis
paragraphs.en: It's a little unusual because just as the mayor talks about this and has made it really central to a lot of his politics and a lot of the politics of these congressional races, there are a ton of other issues. I mean we're always talking about affordability, and somehow this issue has taken what some would call an outsized level of influence when it comes to all of these local races.
paragraphs.cn: 这有点不寻常，因为正如市长所谈论的，并将此作为他许多政治活动以及这些国会竞选政治的核心，还有大量其他问题。我的意思是，我们总是在谈论可负担性，但不知何故，这个问题在所有这些地方竞选中占据了有些人所说的过大影响力。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Is it also because culturally what's happened on the streets, even since Mamdani's election, right, since October 7th, but then since Mamdani's election itself, when you had people saying, oh, we're going to leave the city if he wins, right, raising over these concerns. In particular, I want to flag for you something that happened downtown, I think it was Brooklyn. I'm not sure.
paragraphs.cn: 是否也因为文化上街头发生的事，甚至从曼达尼当选以来，对吧，从10月7日以来，但自从曼达尼当选本身，当时有人说，哦，如果他赢了我们就离开这座城市，对吧，提出了这些担忧。特别是，我想提醒你注意市中心发生的一件事，我想是在布鲁克林。我不太确定。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And you've got this guy, Congressman Goldman. And he's dealing with a bit of a firestorm. He was visiting a coffee shop there. And after he visited, they posted that he was banned over his support for Israel. And they wrote, "we see that you stopped by our shop for coffee. Do you see how it doesn't taste like genocide juice? Or are you still having a hard time telling the difference?" They go on to say, "we don't serve racist, fascists, homophobes, genocide enablers, or anyone in between.
paragraphs.cn: 还有这位众议员戈德曼。他正面临一场风波。他当时去了一家咖啡店。他离开后，店家发帖说他因支持以色列而被禁止入内。他们写道："我们看到你顺道来我们店喝咖啡。你有没有觉得这咖啡尝起来不像种族灭绝的果汁？还是你仍然分不清区别？"他们接着说："我们不服务种族主义者、法西斯分子、恐同者、种族灭绝的帮凶，或任何介于两者之间的人。"

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Too bad we didn't recognize you right away." So, Goldman responded. And here's what he had to say. REP. DAN GOLDMAN (D-NY): And I think it's really dangerous when people start conflating the actions of the Israeli government with American Jews who have absolutely nothing to do with what is going on in Israel and the rise in anti-Semitism, because of what is happening in the Middle East, here in the United States, is really dangerous and it is escalating significantly.
paragraphs.cn: "可惜我们没有立刻认出你。"戈德曼对此做出了回应。以下是他的说法。众议员丹·戈德曼（纽约州民主党）：我认为当人们开始将以色列政府的行为与美国犹太人混为一谈时，这非常危险，美国犹太人与以色列正在发生的事情完全无关，而由于中东正在发生的事情，美国国内反犹太主义的抬头非常危险，并且正在显著升级。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Errol, I don't know if you had heard about this incident --
paragraphs.cn: 埃罗尔，我不知道你是否听说过这件事 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But what do you make of how he is, you know, articulating this?
paragraphs.cn: 但你如何看待他阐述这一点的方式？

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.en: Yes, I did hear about the incident. It's not far from where I'm sitting right now, in fact, in Brooklyn. And very disturbing. Very disturbing. This was -- he went in because he needed to use the bathroom. He was with his seven-year-old daughter while he was out campaigning. And they were nice enough to let him do it. And he bought a cup of coffee. And then after the fact, they published this photo and that statement and that kind of a thing. Listen, the issues in the Middle East are so complicated.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我确实听说了这件事。事实上，离我现在坐的地方不远，就在布鲁克林。非常令人不安。非常令人不安。当时 -- -- 他进去是因为需要上厕所。他带着七岁的女儿外出竞选。他们好心让他用了。他还买了一杯咖啡。事后，他们发布了这张照片和那份声明之类的东西。听着，中东问题非常复杂。

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.en: Issues like genocide, fascism, you know, these buzzwords that some of these activists like to throw around with such abandon are the opposite of trying to find some kind of a path forward and some kind of a civil discourse, which is hopefully what you'll have, including during a political campaign of this kind. It's a real -- really a lost opportunity. But make no mistake about it, this is a group of activists who have declared war on the mainstream Democratic Party.
paragraphs.cn: 像种族灭绝、法西斯主义这样的问题，你知道，这些是一些活动人士喜欢随意抛出的流行词，这与试图找到前进道路和某种文明对话恰恰相反，而这正是人们希望看到的，包括在这种政治竞选期间。这真是一个错失的机会。但不要搞错，这是一群向主流民主党宣战的活动人士。

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.en: And they are using these buzzwords, they are using these issues, that kind of rough style where they try and target people and personalize it is what they're trying to bring to politics. And they say that this is going to be the path forward. We'll see later today, tonight, whether or not New York voters are going to go for that.
paragraphs.cn: 他们正在使用这些流行词，利用这些问题，那种粗暴的风格，试图针对个人并使之个人化，这就是他们试图带入政治的东西。他们说这将是前进的道路。我们将在今天晚些时候、今晚看到纽约选民是否会接受这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's Errol Louis. As always, totally appreciate your time. Thank you. So, we've been talking about this issue, whether this could affect the fate of these politicians in New York. And it appears Israel sealed the fate of Tucker Carlson as well. For months, Republicans have been painting him as anti-Semitic. SEN. TED CRUZ (R-TX): And I will say on the right, I have seen more anti-Semitism in the last 18 months on the right. It is being spread by loud voices, the most consequential of whom is Tucker Carlson.
paragraphs.cn: 这是埃罗尔·路易斯。一如既往，非常感谢你的时间。谢谢。那么，我们一直在讨论这个问题，这是否会影响这些纽约政客的命运。看来以色列也决定了塔克·卡尔森的命运。几个月来，共和党人一直称他为反犹太主义者。参议员特德·克鲁兹（得克萨斯州共和党）：我要说的是，在右翼，过去18个月我看到了更多的反犹太主义。它是由一些响亮的声音传播的，其中最重要的是塔克·卡尔森。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I believe Tucker Carlson is the single most dangerous demagogue in this country.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为塔克·卡尔森是这个国家最危险的煽动者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Carlson says he is done with the Republican Party. He says it puts the interest of Israel above those of its own citizens.
paragraphs.cn: 卡尔森表示他与共和党已经决裂。他说该党将以色列的利益置于本国公民之上。

paragraphs.speaker: Tucker Carlson
paragraphs.en: I would not support the Republican Party. There's no chance I would support the Republican Party. I'm not going to support the Democratic Party. I don't know what I'm going to do. But at this point, you know, how could you support -- how could I or any American voter support a political party that's not loyal to the United States?
paragraphs.cn: 我不会支持共和党。我绝无可能支持共和党。我也不会支持民主党。我不知道自己会怎么做。但此时此刻，你怎么能 -- -- 我或任何美国选民怎么能支持一个不忠于美国的政党？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Group chat is back. Tam, we're going two ends of the spectrum here, right?
paragraphs.cn: 群聊回来了。塔姆，我们俩的观点处于光谱两端，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: And then it meets up at the top.
paragraphs.cn: 然后它们会在顶端交汇。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The horseshoe politics. I think what's interesting is that Tucker has been at odds with the White House for many months almost every time there's a foreign intervention. But this one in particular seems to have pushed him over the edge in terms of how he speaks publicly.
paragraphs.cn: 马蹄铁政治。我觉得有趣的是，塔克与白宫意见相左已有数月，几乎每次有外国干预时都是如此。但这一次尤其让他公开表态时显得更加激进。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: I think that what is happening here with Tucker Carlson, with Ted Cruz is various parts of what has been the Republican coalition figuring out what happens in a potential post-Trump reality and positioning themselves. And there are flashpoints. Israel is going to be a flashpoint. It didn't used to be. Republicans were all very pro-Israel. That's not the case anymore. And I think you're going to see it over abortion and other issues as well.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为塔克·卡尔森和特德·克鲁兹正在做的事情，是共和党联盟的不同派系在探索后特朗普时代的潜在现实，并为自己定位。而有一些引爆点。以色列将成为一个引爆点。过去并非如此。共和党人曾一致强烈支持以色列。但现在情况不同了。我认为你还会在堕胎及其他问题上看到类似情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeting, "Tucker's not the only one who's done supporting the Republican Party." This is her, I think, on X. "There's a lot of us that are absolutely fed up and will not support a party that betrays its voters and country. That doesn't mean we're turning into Democrats either. But we are done with the America last Republican Party."
paragraphs.cn: 是的。玛乔丽·泰勒·格林发推文说："塔克不是唯一一个不再支持共和党的人。"我认为她是在X平台上说的。"我们中有很多人已经受够了，不会支持一个背叛选民和国家的政党。这并不意味着我们要变成民主党人。但我们受够了'美国最后'的共和党。"

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: There's something going on here. It's not just this issue. If you think about this issue, you think about the war. You think about the Epstein files. You have all of these activists on the right who voted for Donald Trump because of those three things. And Tucker Carlson, major -- maybe Marjorie Taylor Greene, are trying to tap into that. The old political consultant here thinks it has a lot to do with 2028.
paragraphs.cn: 这里有些情况在发生。不仅仅是这个问题。想想这个问题，想想战争，想想爱泼斯坦文件。右翼有很多活动人士当初投票给唐纳德·特朗普就是因为这三件事。而塔克·卡尔森 -- -- 或许还有玛乔丽·泰勒·格林 -- -- 正试图利用这一点。这里的老政治顾问认为这与2028年有很大关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And, yes, 2028, and also where the public sentiment is. I mean when I went to look at the Pew research polls, I was trying to figure out, OK, what are the views of Israel right now based on the polling?
paragraphs.cn: 是的，2028年，还有公众情绪。我的意思是，当我查看皮尤研究中心的民调时，我想弄清楚，根据民调，目前对以色列的看法是怎样的？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And in 2022, Republicans were at 27 percent with an unfavorable view of Israel. Now they're at 41, which means it's almost doubled. And meanwhile, Democrats, of course, we know, in 2022, 53 percent. Now they're at 80 percent. So, a Mamdani or a Tucker, they're speaking to the people who moved in this shift from 2022 to now.
paragraphs.cn: 2022年，共和党人对以色列持负面看法的比例为27%。现在达到了41%，几乎翻了一番。与此同时，民主党人，当然，我们知道，2022年是53%，现在达到了80%。所以，无论是马姆达尼还是塔克，他们都在对从2022年到现在发生转变的人群发声。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Israel is what they're fighting right now. But I think it's very important to say they're against any sort of foreign conflict.
paragraphs.cn: 以色列是他们现在正在对抗的目标。但我认为很重要的一点是，他们反对任何形式的外部冲突。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: I don't think it's just Israel. I think it would be, if we were -- obviously, no one on the Republican side wanted us to be in Ukraine either. So, I think that's -- but I agree with Chuck in regards to --
paragraphs.cn: 我不认为只是以色列。我认为如果是 -- -- 显然，共和党方面也没有人希望我们介入乌克兰。所以，我认为那是 -- -- 但我同意查克关于 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But he's not leaving over Venezuela. Like, it feels as though --
paragraphs.cn: 但他并不是因为委内瑞拉而离开。感觉好像 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: No, but we're not at war --
paragraphs.cn: 不，但我们没有处于战争状态 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: I mean that was like something very quick. We don't have a lot of troops on the ground. You know, we're not -- anyway, I think that's a little bit different. But I get your point. I also think that Marjorie Taylor Greene and Tucker are trying to figure out a path in regards to 2028. I think they're both testing the grounds of running. But also one of the questions I have really for you is, there are four or five potential Jewish candidates on the Democrat side that would run in 2028.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是那件事发生得非常快。我们在当地没有很多部队。你知道，我们不是 -- -- 总之，我认为那有点不同。但我明白你的意思。我还认为玛乔丽·泰勒·格林和塔克正在试图为2028年找到一条道路。我认为他们都在试探参选的可能性。但我真正想问你的一个问题是，民主党方面有四到五位潜在的犹太裔候选人可能会在2028年参选。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: One of them, my dear friend Josh Shapiro. How does that happen? I mean, they're like going -- trying -- the Dems are trying to go left of the left of the left of Israel.
paragraphs.cn: 其中之一是我亲爱的朋友乔什·夏皮罗。这怎么会发生？我的意思是，他们正在 -- -- 试图 -- -- 民主党人试图走向比以色列更左、更左、更左的位置。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: This has -- this has relevance in Democratic and primaries for both parties in 2028. When you have multiple people running, more than four or five, you can win the nomination with 30 percent. Keep in mind, you just need that 30 percent. And Tucker Carlson knows that.
paragraphs.cn: 这在2028年两党的民主党初选中具有相关性。当有多人竞选，超过四到五人时，你可以用30%的选票赢得提名。请记住，你只需要那30%。塔克·卡尔森知道这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. You get -- you think he's -- this is a he's running situation? OK. Well, let's talk about what else is in our group chats. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: 好的。你认为 -- -- 你认为他是在参选的情况？好的。那么，让我们谈谈我们群聊中还有什么。（交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Starting with you, Ashley.
paragraphs.cn: 从你开始，阿什莉。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: I'm talking about the power pivot. OK, I just want to talk about how great it is to watch all of these people visiting from Europe or around the world for the soccer teams.
paragraphs.cn: 我在谈论权力转移。好的，我只想谈谈看到所有这些来自欧洲或世界各地的人为足球队而来访是多么棒。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. World Cup fans.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。世界杯球迷们。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: I mean, but also loving America. I mean they're like really loving our food. I have heard that a lot of people are gaining some weight.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，他们也热爱美国。他们真的很喜欢我们的食物。我听说很多人都在长胖。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: We are making them fat.
paragraphs.cn: 我们把他们喂胖了。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: So, we may need to give them some (INAUDIBLE) on the way out.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我们可能需要在送他们离开时给他们一些（听不清）。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I had not heard that. But, thank you. Ashley.
paragraphs.cn: 我没听说过。不过，谢谢你。阿什莉。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Oh, yes. No, people are gaining lots of weight. You know, if you're in the Midwest, you're eating a lot of gravy. Yeah. Wait until you see until they get to Costco. That's all their minds will be blown. But it is nice. It's been a lot of compliments.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，是的。不，很多人都在长胖。你知道，如果你在中西部，你会吃很多肉汁。是啊。等他们到了好市多，你就会看到。他们的脑子都会炸开的。但这很好。我们收到了很多赞美。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Just wait till you see -- until they get to Costco and their minds will be blown.
paragraphs.cn: 等他们到了好市多，他们的脑子都会炸开的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, that's a whole other situation. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，那完全是另一回事了。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: But it is nice. It's been a lot of complements.
paragraphs.cn: 但这很好。我们收到了很多赞美。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: So today I'm really honored. I get to host the first annual Latino Vote Summit. We're bringing in Republican, independent, and Democrat Latinos to the press club today where we're going to talk about the Latino agenda. If you're a Republican, holding the Republican Party accountable. If you're a Democrat, holding -- but what is it for the community?
paragraphs.cn: 所以今天我非常荣幸。我将主持首届拉丁裔投票峰会。我们今天邀请了共和党、独立派和民主党的拉丁裔人士来到新闻俱乐部，讨论拉丁裔议程。如果你是共和党人，就要让共和党负责。如果你是民主党人，也要让民主党负责 -- -- 但这对社区来说意味着什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: So, I'm really happy that we're coming together.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我很高兴我们能够聚在一起。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Because 2024 revealed some big rifts.
paragraphs.cn: 因为2024年暴露了一些巨大的裂痕。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: The Latino vote has probably been the one electorate that swung right, swung left, and now they're in the middle looking for a home. And I'm honored to be hosting this with my Latino vote podcast co-host Mike Madrid.
paragraphs.cn: 拉丁裔选民可能是唯一一个摇摆不定、时而右倾时而左倾的选民群体，现在他们处于中间地带，寻找归属。我很荣幸能与我的拉丁裔选民播客联合主持人迈克·马德里一起主持这个节目。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Very nice. Very nice. Shout out to the pod. OK, Tamara, for you?
paragraphs.cn: 很好。很好。向播客致敬。好的，塔玛拉，你呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Oh, yes, finally. Thank you, Tamara.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，是的，终于来了。谢谢你，塔玛拉。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Yes. What I cannot let go of is this Reflecting Pool. The ongoing battle. The --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我无法释怀的是这个倒影池。这场持续的战斗。 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Are you hunting for vandals?
paragraphs.cn: 你在追捕破坏者吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: I am not hunting for vandals.
paragraphs.cn: 我没有在追捕破坏者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Is Bill Pulte hunting for vandals?
paragraphs.cn: 比尔·普尔特在追捕破坏者吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: It could be part of the intel job. Who knows?
paragraphs.cn: 这可能是情报工作的一部分。谁知道呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: It could be part of the remit.
paragraphs.cn: 这可能是职责的一部分。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Keith
paragraphs.en: But, you know, finding out what happened. Epoxy. It's tough. So is keeping a pool clean.
paragraphs.cn: 但是，你知道，查明真相。环氧树脂。这很难。保持泳池清洁也一样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, really? Do you have a problem with this?
paragraphs.cn: 哦，真的吗？你觉得这有问题吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: I no longer have a pool.
paragraphs.cn: 我已经没有泳池了。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: Was it -- like what --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Did you say the president (ph)?
paragraphs.cn: 你刚才是说总统吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: What happened to it?
paragraphs.cn: 它怎么了？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You have to clean a pool. It's annoying.
paragraphs.cn: 你得清理泳池。很烦人。

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.en: I know. And you didn't. Is that why you got rid of it? Was it like an above ground swimming pool?
paragraphs.cn: 我知道。而你没有。这就是你把它处理掉的原因吗？是那种地上泳池吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: It was -- it was just a lot of work.
paragraphs.cn: 它 -- -- 它只是太费事了。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Yes, it had all kinds of problems.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，它存在各种问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: It's hard to keep the chemicals right.
paragraphs.cn: 很难保持化学物质的比例正确。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Did you catch who did it?
paragraphs.cn: 你发现是谁干的了吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Keith
paragraphs.en: Me and my indifference to chemicals.
paragraphs.cn: 我和我对化学物质的漠不关心。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Well, thank you guys so much for talking with me today. We found -- we uncovered a lot of very serious news that we need to follow up on.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。非常感谢你们今天和我交谈。我们发现 -- -- 我们揭露了很多需要跟进的重磅新闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: But we did not find out who sabotaged the pool.
paragraphs.cn: 但我们没有查出是谁破坏了游泳池。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We have not found that out yet.
paragraphs.cn: 我们还没有查出来。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Right. That's what I'm worried about.
paragraphs.cn: 对，这正是我担心的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We will have intelligence agencies on that shortly.
paragraphs.cn: 我们很快就会让情报机构介入调查。

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: They put up new cameras down there all up and down the way.
paragraphs.cn: 他们在那里沿途都安装了新的摄像头。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, see, this is how you're getting me in trouble. Everybody else, especially those of you in New York, good luck today. Polls are open. Go out and vote. We've got plenty of news for you coming up, as we said, about the Reflecting Pool, about the state of the Iran talks, Rubio heading to the Middle East. Those headlines are next. I'm Audie Cornish, and thank you for waking up with me. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 好了，你看，这就是你让我惹上麻烦的方式。其他人，尤其是纽约的各位，祝你们今天好运。投票站已经开放，出去投票吧。我们接下来还有很多新闻要带给你们，正如我们所说，关于倒影池、伊朗谈判的进展，以及鲁比奥前往中东。这些头条新闻即将呈现。我是奥迪·科尼什，感谢你和我一起醒来。

vocabulary.en: the day when primary elections are held
vocabulary.excerpt: it's primary day in New York
vocabulary.exampleCn: 今天是纽约初选日。

vocabulary.en: a person who advocates for democratic socialism, combining socialist economic policies with democratic political systems
vocabulary.excerpt: Can more Democratic socialists ride Mayor Mamdani's wave?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 更多民主社会主义者能乘着马姆达尼市长的浪潮获胜吗？

vocabulary.en: to take advantage of a trend or momentum
vocabulary.excerpt: hoping to ride the wave of Democratic socialists
vocabulary.exampleCn: 希望乘着民主社会主义者的浪潮。

vocabulary.en: the current holder of a political office
vocabulary.excerpt: Two are going up against Democratic incumbents
vocabulary.exampleCn: 两人正挑战民主党现任议员。

vocabulary.en: the period during which someone holds a position
vocabulary.excerpt: tested just six months into his tenure
vocabulary.exampleCn: 在他上任仅六个月时受到考验。

vocabulary.en: a list of candidates for election
vocabulary.excerpt: This slate here today is our answer.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 今天这份候选人名单就是我们的回答。

vocabulary.en: favoring social reform and progressive policies
vocabulary.excerpt: I'm a longtime progressive
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我是一名长期的进步派。

vocabulary.en: the political system dominated by established parties and interests
vocabulary.excerpt: felt deeply abandoned by the establishment politics
vocabulary.exampleCn: 深感被建制派政治抛弃。

vocabulary.en: factors that help progress or success
vocabulary.excerpt: he is trying to have, like, tailwinds
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他试图拥有顺风助力。

vocabulary.en: to extend or project known information to unknown situations
vocabulary.excerpt: I don't know that we can extrapolate about the national Democratic Party
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我不知道我们能否推断出全国民主党的情况。

vocabulary.en: to succeed despite difficulties or low probability
vocabulary.excerpt: three Democratic socialists overcame the odds
vocabulary.exampleCn: 三位民主社会主义者逆袭成功。

vocabulary.cn: 战场选区，竞争激烈的选区
vocabulary.en: a electoral district where the outcome is uncertain and both parties compete heavily
vocabulary.excerpt: There's about 34 battleground districts
vocabulary.exampleCn: 大约有34个战场选区。

vocabulary.cn: 边缘席位，胜负难分的席位
vocabulary.en: a parliamentary seat held by a small majority, easily lost
vocabulary.excerpt: in these marginal seats
vocabulary.exampleCn: 在这些边缘席位中。

vocabulary.en: to separate or disassociate
vocabulary.excerpt: You're trying to divorce it here.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 你试图在这里把它分开。

vocabulary.cn: 创造性紧张，建设性冲突
vocabulary.en: a productive conflict that leads to positive change
vocabulary.excerpt: Dr. King talked about creative tension being a catalyst for social justice
vocabulary.exampleCn: 马丁·路德·金博士谈到创造性紧张是社会正义的催化剂。

vocabulary.cn: 扩大阵营，包容更多群体
vocabulary.en: to make a political party or movement more inclusive
vocabulary.excerpt: we are broadening the tent of the Democratic Party
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们正在扩大民主党的阵营。

vocabulary.en: opposed to the established social, political, or economic system
vocabulary.excerpt: it's really anti-establishment
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这实际上是反建制的。

vocabulary.en: the existing state of affairs
vocabulary.excerpt: running against the status quo
vocabulary.exampleCn: 反对现状。

vocabulary.en: general election
vocabulary.excerpt: can they win in a general?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们能在普选中获胜吗？

vocabulary.en: a type of independent political action committee that can raise unlimited funds
vocabulary.excerpt: super PACs at large who are spending millions of dollars
vocabulary.exampleCn: 超级政治行动委员会花费数百万美元。

vocabulary.en: to cover completely
vocabulary.excerpt: deceptive and misleading ads that are blanketing airwaves
vocabulary.exampleCn: 铺天盖地的欺骗性和误导性广告。

vocabulary.cn: 令人恐惧的人或事物，妖怪
vocabulary.en: a person or thing that is widely feared or blamed
vocabulary.excerpt: AIPAC has become a real boogeyman in races
vocabulary.exampleCn: AIPAC已成为竞选中的真正妖怪。

vocabulary.en: a serious disagreement or split
vocabulary.excerpt: created a political rift
vocabulary.exampleCn: 造成了政治裂痕。

vocabulary.en: to make a problem or situation worse
vocabulary.excerpt: exacerbated by October 7th
vocabulary.exampleCn: 因10月7日事件而加剧。

sentences.en: And imagine opening your door and finding two million reasons to stay inside.
sentences.cn: 想象一下，打开门发现有两百万个理由让你待在屋里。
sentences.structure: 祈使句 + 并列动名词短语作宾语
sentences.analysis: ‘two million reasons’ 是比喻，指两百万只蜜蜂；‘reasons to stay inside’ 是名词短语，不定式作定语。

sentences.en: Video from Queen Bee Supply shows the chaos workers faced to capture the swarm.
sentences.cn: Queen Bee Supply 的视频展示了工人在捕捉蜂群时面临的混乱场面。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 省略关系代词的定语从句 ‘workers faced’ + 不定式短语 ‘to capture the swarm’
sentences.analysis: ‘the chaos workers faced’ 是省略 that 的定语从句；‘to capture the swarm’ 表示目的。

sentences.en: Can more Democratic socialists ride Mayor Mamdani's wave?
sentences.cn: 更多的民主社会主义者能否乘着曼达尼市长的浪潮前进？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句，主语 ‘more Democratic socialists’，谓语 ‘ride’，宾语 ‘Mayor Mamdani's wave’
sentences.analysis: ‘ride the wave’ 是习语，意为‘乘势而上’；‘Mayor Mamdani's wave’ 指曼达尼带来的政治势头。

sentences.en: Will justices go against the president in cases that could help define his power?
sentences.cn: 法官们会在可能界定总统权力的案件中反对总统吗？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句 + 定语从句 ‘that could help define his power’ 修饰 ‘cases’
sentences.analysis: ‘go against’ 意为‘反对’；‘define his power’ 指界定总统的权力范围。

sentences.en: And so-called deep-state firings have started at a top U.S. intelligence agency.
sentences.cn: 所谓的‘深层政府’解雇行动已在美国一家顶级情报机构开始。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语 ‘so-called deep-state firings’，谓语 ‘have started’
sentences.analysis: ‘so-called’ 表示‘所谓的’；‘deep-state’ 指‘深层政府’，即被认为暗中操控政府的势力。

sentences.en: What else could the president's hand-picked temporary director do?
sentences.cn: 总统亲自挑选的临时主管还能做什么？
sentences.structure: 特殊疑问句，主语 ‘the president's hand-picked temporary director’，谓语 ‘could do’
sentences.analysis: ‘hand-picked’ 意为‘精心挑选的’；‘temporary director’ 指临时主管。

sentences.en: He's backed candidates in three House races.
sentences.cn: 他支持了三个众议院席位的候选人。
sentences.structure: 现在完成时简单句，主语 ‘He’，谓语 ‘has backed’，宾语 ‘candidates’，状语 ‘in three House races’
sentences.analysis: ‘back’ 作动词意为‘支持’；‘House races’ 指众议院选举。

sentences.en: Two are going up against Democratic incumbents, and they are hoping to ride the wave of Democratic socialists, who have been winning big races across the country.
sentences.cn: 两人正与民主党现任议员竞争，他们希望乘着民主社会主义者的浪潮前进，这些人在全国范围内赢得了重大选举。
sentences.structure: 并列句 + 非限制性定语从句 ‘who have been winning...’ 修饰 ‘Democratic socialists’
sentences.analysis: ‘go up against’ 意为‘对抗’；‘ride the wave’ 同上；‘incumbents’ 指现任者。

sentences.en: I am someone who has felt deeply abandoned by the establishment politics that far too often see my community as merely a statistic.
sentences.cn: 我是一个深感被建制政治抛弃的人，这种政治常常把我的社区仅仅视为一个统计数据。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 定语从句 ‘who has felt...’ + 嵌套定语从句 ‘that far too often see...’ 修饰 ‘establishment politics’
sentences.analysis: ‘establishment politics’ 指‘建制派政治’；‘see...as merely a statistic’ 意为‘仅仅视为一个数字’。

sentences.en: They're not handpicked by the party, yet somehow, they rise via the Internet and the power of their ideas.
sentences.cn: 他们并非由政党精心挑选，但不知何故，他们通过互联网和思想的力量崛起。
sentences.structure: 并列句，由 ‘yet’ 连接，前后分句均为简单句
